globalGate.wave: COORD / Coordination Fittings (Beads + mcp_agent_mail + planning gate) for cross-session drift prevention
globalGate.summary: All 7 COORD slices passed. Two coordination tools added as Fittings (coord-beads, coord-agentmail) + a Garrison-owned MIT planning-gate MCP (coord-mcp) + the digest/nudge hook + the coord observability CLI. Committed gates: 7 coord test files = 51 passed + 1 gated-live skip; typecheck 0; lint clean; build exit 0 (after clean .next). Cross-model: every slice has a Codex approve (CO1 r1->r3, CO2 r1->r2, CO3 r1->r5 [5-round concurrency hardening], CO4 r1, CO5 r1->r2, CO6 r1->r2). LIVE machine-wide wiring verified: `claude mcp list` on the real ~/.claude shows coord-mcp + coord-agentmail Connected from a fresh non-Garrison repo (direct path) AND a composition cwd (orchestrator path), existing user MCP servers untouched. PTY-safe (all hooks command-type, no claude -p/Agent-SDK). License-isolation: agent_mail at ~/.garrison/external (outside MIT tree), zero imports, kept out of Codex scope, not wired to Ekoa.

crossModel.pwTestAllPassed: n/a (backend/CLI slices — no UI to drive; correctness gate is committed vitest + live transport/demo)

globalGate.preExistingBaseline: 7 full-suite failures unrelated to this wave + unaffected by it: removed memory seed (claude-install, seed x3, validation, fitting-files-api) + 1 documented gemini-runtime buildArgs baseline. Zero failing tests reference coord; runner tests green.
